top_signals.title: Pakistan Bypasses Afghanistan With New Corridors Through Iran and China
top_signals.summary: Pakistan has approved and operationalized new land routes to connect Central Asian markets to Pakistani ports and beyond, utilizing strategic corridors through Iran and China to bypass Afghanistan entirely. This shift was solidified in April 2026 when Pakistan Customs launched the first export consignment from the Karachi Export Processing Zone to Kyrgyzstan via the Sost Dry Port in China under the TIR (Transports Internationaux Routiers) regime. Pakistan’s decision to diversify transit away from Afghanistan follows the indefinite closure…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Jessica Noviskis on Market Uncertainty Surrounding AI Rally
top_signals.summary: Marquette Associates OCIO Portfolio Strategist Jessica Noviskis, discusses the current market environment and how Marquette Associates is investing. She speak with Guy Johnson on July 3, 2026. (Source: Bloomberg)

top_signals.title: Germany to Sweeten Terms of Gas Plant Auction to Draw More Bids
top_signals.summary: Germany will lift the maximum amount it will pay developers in upcoming auctions for gas-fired power plants as it seeks to attract more bids for urgently needed back-up power capacity.

top_signals.title: Terminal Portuario de Guayaquil Surpasses 2,200 Hours of Simulated Port Training
top_signals.summary: In the first half of 2026, Terminal Portuario de Guayaquil (TPG), a Hanseatic Global Terminals port, has already accumulated more than 1,000 hours of training for reachstacker operators and more than 1,200 hours for STS and RTG crane operators…

top_signals.title: The 250-year history of U.S. energy consumption
top_signals.summary: Over the 250-year history of our nation, energy consumption has evolved from wood use in the 18th and 19th centuries to today's use of modern renewable, hydrocarbon, and nuclear technology. In 2025, total energy used in the United States was 96 quadrillion British thermal units (quads), up 2% from 2024, but below 2007's record 99 quads. Petroleum was the most-used energy source last year, followed closely by natural gas. Use of renewable, coal, and nuclear energy each made up about 9% of total energy use.

alerts.title: The 250-year history of U.S. energy consumption
alerts.summary: Over the 250-year history of our nation, energy consumption has evolved from wood use in the 18th and 19th centuries to today's use of modern renewable, hydrocarbon, and nuclear technology. In 2025, total energy used in the United States was 96 quadrillion British thermal units (quads), up 2% from 2024, but below 2007's record 99 quads. Petroleum was the most-used energy source last year, followed closely by natural gas. Use of renewable, coal, and nuclear energy each made up about 9% of total energy use.
